prompt: We were debating the best late-night food spot and he sent 'I'll have to take you to my secret taco place and prove you wrong'. How do I playfully accept without seeming too eager?
She sent a screenshot of her Spotify wrapped and we have the exact same top artist. What's a witty way to reply?
He replied to my Instagram story (me at a concert) with 'looks loud but you look great'. How do I keep the banter going?
I playfully told him his sweater makes him look like a sophisticated grandfather, and he replied 'A grandfather who could steal your girl'. Need a sharp comeback.
She casually mentioned that she is 'accepting applications for a plus one to a wedding next month'. Should I shoot my shot?
We just matched on Hinge and his prompt says 'Let's debate the most overrated pasta shape'. I want to start strong.
I sent a voice note enthusiastically talking about a promotion I just got at work. He replied 4 hours later with 'That’s awesome man congrats'.
We had a really good second date, but since then she’s been taking almost a full day to reply to texts, and her responses are just one line.
I asked if he preferred Friday or Saturday for getting drinks, and he just said 'let me check my schedule' and hasn't followed up in two days.
I tried to start a conversation about a show we both watch, and she just sent 'yeah that episode was crazy'.
He usually uses a lot of emojis and exclamation points, but today when I asked about his weekend plans he just said 'Not much just chilling'.
I texted her 'had a great time tonight!' right after our first date, and she reacted to the message with a heart but didn't actually reply.
I accidentally sent a screenshot of our conversation to him instead of my group chat. He just saw it.
I got a little too drunk last night and double-texted her complaining about my ex. Woke up to silence.
I made a sarcastic joke about her job that came off way harsher over text than I meant. She said 'wow okay'.
He told me he couldn't hang out because he was exhausted, and I saw him tagged at a bar on a friend's story. I passive-aggressively texted 'hope you're resting well!'
We were supposed to meet at 7. I fell asleep and didn't wake up until 8:30. He texted 'Guess we aren't doing this?' at 7:45.
I panicked when she asked what I was looking for, and I said 'just keeping it super casual' even though I actually really like her.
We've been dating for a month and I haven't heard from him all weekend. I'm feeling really anxious and just want to know he's thinking of me without sounding needy.
I saw he updated his dating app profile with new photos even though we've been seeing each other exclusively for 3 weeks.
She brought up her ex randomly in conversation today and it made me feel like a rebound.
I really miss him today. We had a great weekend together but haven't texted much this week because of work.
It's her birthday next week (we've been dating 2 months). What's a good way to ask what her plans are without making her feel obligated to spend it with me?
He said 'we should definitely get dinner soon' but didn't propose a specific day. I want to lock it down but don't want to seem pushy.
I want to invite him over to my place to cook dinner for our fourth date, but I don't want it to automatically imply we're sleeping together.
I had to cancel our last date because I got sick, and I want to ask him out again to make up for it.
He just sent me a link to a song with no caption.
She texted 'We need to talk about what happened last night.' I honestly have no idea what she's referring to.
Out of nowhere after 3 months of silence, my ex just texted 'Hey, random question for you...'
He replied 'You're something else lol' to my last text.
